questions.question: In MOET, the fertilized eggs are recovered non-surgically and transferred to surrogate mothers at which stage?

questions.solution: By definition, Oligodendrocytes performs this role.

questions.question: Cross-breeding gives rise to new breeds. For example, Bikaneri ewes and Marino rams were crossed to form a new sheep breed in which state?
Which biological phenomenon explains why a single out-cross can restore the fertility and yield that was lost due to continuous inbreeding?
Which of the following is considered the most critical factor for successful apiculture (bee-keeping)?

options.A: Knowledge of the nature and habits of bees
options.B: Feeding bees with artificial nectar exclusively
options.C: Keeping bee hives in totally dark, tightly sealed rooms
options.D: Rearing only the drone bees for honey production

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Aquaculture and Pisciculture?

options.A: Pisciculture focuses only on fish, whereas Aquaculture includes fish, crustaceans, molluscs, and aquatic plants
options.B: Pisciculture is for marine organisms, while Aquaculture is for freshwater organisms
options.C: Aquaculture only refers to aquatic plants, separating it from fish farming
options.D: They are perfectly synonymous with no distinction

questions.question: Myelin sheath in CNS is formed by: (Exam form 698)
questions.solution: By definition, Oligodendrocytes performs this role.

questions.question: Which of the following marine fishes is widely consumed and forms a major part of India's coastal economy?
If a breeder wishes to develop a high-yielding, disease-resistant variety of cattle quickly, they should ideally employ a combination of which techniques?

options.A: Artificial Insemination and Multiple Ovulation Embryo Transfer (MOET)

questions.question: Which of the following is the most abundant component of a standard, well-formulated feed for dairy cattle?
